response: Originating in the 1960s as a US military project and expanding with the 1969 ARPANET launch and 1989 invention of the World Wide Web, the internet has evolved from a niche research network into a ubiquitous global infrastructure. Its public accessibility and commercial power exploded through the 1990s dot-com boom, the rise of social media, and the 2007 smartphone revolution. Today, this foundational technology connects over 5 billion people worldwide, underpinning virtually every aspect of modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
